name: Devlin et al., 'BERT', NAACL 2019

task.description: Create a self-contained 1920x1080 HTML slide titled 'BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers (Devlin et al., 2019)'. The body is TWO equal-width side-by-side panels with identical top and bottom edges and a uniform central gutter: LEFT panel header 'Pre-training' shows an inline-SVG flow 'Unlabeled text -> [MLM + NSP objectives] -> BERT encoder (12 or 24 layers)' with two bullets ('Masked LM: mask 15% of tokens, predict them', 'Next Sentence Prediction: is B the next sentence?'); RIGHT panel header 'Fine-tuning' shows a flow 'Pre-trained BERT -> + task head -> labeled task data' with two bullets ('Same architecture, swap the top layer', 'One model -> 11 NLP tasks'). BELOW the two panels, spanning the full width, a single results strip: a 5-cell equal-width row of GLUE-style stat cards 'GLUE 80.5', 'MNLI 86.7', 'SQuAD v1.1 F1 93.2', 'SQuAD v2.0 F1 83.1', 'SWAG 86.3'. A footnote at the bottom. ALSO, below the GLUE strip, add a full-width input-representation diagram: a horizontal row of 6 token cells where each token shows three stacked summed embeddings (Token + Segment + Position), all token cells equal width and aligned.
requirements: The two main panels are equal width and share the same top and bottom edge
There is a visible uniform gutter between the two panels (>=24px), and neither panel's content crosses into the other
The five GLUE stat cards form one row of equal-width cards with uniform gaps between them
All five stat cards show both a label and its number, none clipped
No element overlaps any other element anywhere on the slide
Each bullet is at most two lines and fully inside its panel
The two inline-SVG flow diagrams are fully inside their respective panels (no overflow)
Nothing overflows the 1920x1080 canvas; no scrollbar
The footnote is fully visible above the bottom edge and overlaps nothing
Every text element is at least 12px
The input-representation row shows 6 equal-width token cells, each with three stacked embedding layers, aligned in one row

ss_html:
BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers
(Devlin et al., 2019)

Pre-training

Unlabeled text → [MLM + NSP objectives] → BERT encoder
(12 or 24 layers)
  • Masked LM: mask 15% of tokens, predict them
  • Next Sentence Prediction: is B the next sentence?

Fine-tuning

Pre-trained BERT → + task head → labeled task data
  • Same architecture, swap the top layer
  • One model → 11 NLP tasks
GLUE
80.5
MNLI
86.7
SQuAD v1.1 F1
93.2
SQuAD v2.0 F1
83.1
SWAG
86.3

Input Representation: Token + Segment + Position Embeddings

[CLS]
Token
+
Segment A
+
Position 0
my
Token
+
Segment A
+
Position 1
dog
Token
+
Segment A
+
Position 2
[SEP]
Token
+
Segment A
+
Position 3
it
Token
+
Segment B
+
Position 4
[SEP]
Token
+
Segment B
+
Position 5
BERT revolutionized NLP by introducing bidirectional context understanding through masked language modeling and achieving state-of-the-art results across multiple benchmarks.

GLUE results strip (5 cards) 4. Input representation diagram (6 token cells with 3 stacked embeddings each) 5. Footnote at bottom Let me plan the heights: - Total: 1080px - Title: ~80px - Panels: ~320px - GLUE strip: ~110px - Input representation: ~160px - Footnote: ~30px - Padding/margins: let me calculate carefully Now I'm working out the precise spacing with 32px top padding, 20px side margins, and 16px bottom padding. The title takes 70px, then I'm allocating 16px gaps between each major section—panels at 300px, GLUE strip at 110px, input representation at 150px, and finally the footnote. Adding this up gives me 770px, which is short of the 1080px height, so I need to recalculate. Let me work with the full 1920x1080 viewport and account for 36px outer padding on all sides, leaving 1008px of usable height. I'm increasing the component sizes—title to 72px, panels to 280px, GLUE strip to 120px, input diagram to 170px, and footnote to 32px—but that still only gets me to 738px, so I need to expand these further to fill the space properly. That gives me almost exactly 1008 pixels with the adjusted spacing. Now I'm settling on a color scheme with a dark navy background, blue as the primary accent, and amber for secondary highlights, keeping the text clean and readable against the darker panels. Writing the HTML layout... Writing the HTML structure... Writing layout structure... I've found the right proportions—80px for the title, 22px gaps between major sections, 420px for the panels, 150px for the GLUE strip, and 260px for the input area gets me to exactly 1026px within my 1028px available space. trace.feedback:
Quality score: 70%
DOES NOT MEET REQUIREMENTS

Issues found:
  1. [MAJOR] The two main panels (Pre-training and Fine-tuning) have unequal widths - the left panel appears narrower than the right panel (at center)
  2. [MAJOR] The input representation section shows only 6 token cells instead of the required layout, and the embedding layers are not clearly stacked in three distinct layers (at bottom)
  3. [MINOR] Some text in the bullet points appears to be very close to the minimum 12px requirement and may be difficult to read (at left panel)

Suggested fixes:
  1. Make the two main panels equal width by adjusting the layout grid
  2. Ensure the input representation section clearly shows 6 equal-width token cells with three distinct embedding layers stacked vertically
  3. Increase font size for bullet point text to ensure better readability
  4. Add more consistent spacing between the main panels to create a clearer visual separation
